test_cases.title: Sort products Name (A to Z)
test_cases.description: Selecting the 'Name (A to Z)' sort orders the product names in ascending alphabetical order.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Saucedemo sorts client-side in the browser; there is no backend sort endpoint to assert against, so 'works correctly' can only be verified by reading the rendered product order after selecting the option. This is a UI-only behavior with no lower-level seam.
preconditions: BASE_URL points at Saucedemo; a standard_user session is on the inventory page.

steps.action: Select the 'Name (A to Z)' option in the product sort dropdown.
Read the list of product names in display order.

expected_results: The product names are in ascending alphabetical (A->Z) order.

test_cases.title: Sort products Name (Z to A)
test_cases.description: Selecting the 'Name (Z to A)' sort orders the product names in descending alphabetical order.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Client-side sort, no backend contract; the descending order is only observable in the rendered UI after selecting the option.
preconditions: BASE_URL points at Saucedemo; a standard_user session is on the inventory page.

steps.action: Select the 'Name (Z to A)' option in the product sort dropdown.
Read the list of product names in display order.

expected_results: The product names are in descending alphabetical (Z->A) order.

test_cases.title: Sort products Price (low to high)
test_cases.description: Selecting the 'Price (low to high)' sort orders products by ascending numeric price (guards against string-based price comparison).
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Client-side sort with no backend seam. P0 because incorrect price ordering directly misleads purchasing; the assertion must parse prices as numbers (not strings) to catch the RISK-002 '$10 before $9' bug, which is only observable end-to-end.
preconditions: BASE_URL points at Saucedemo; a standard_user session is on the inventory page.

steps.action: Select the 'Price (low to high)' option in the product sort dropdown.
Read the product prices in display order and parse them as numbers.

expected_results: The numeric prices are in ascending order (each price >= the previous one).

test_cases.title: Sort products Price (high to low)
test_cases.description: Selecting the 'Price (high to low)' sort orders products by descending numeric price.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Client-side sort with no backend seam. P0 for the same purchasing-impact reason as TC-003; the assertion parses prices numerically to verify true descending order.
preconditions: BASE_URL points at Saucedemo; a standard_user session is on the inventory page.

steps.action: Select the 'Price (high to low)' option in the product sort dropdown.
Read the product prices in display order and parse them as numbers.

expected_results: The numeric prices are in descending order (each price <= the previous one).

test_cases.title: Selected sort option remains visible as the active selection
test_cases.description: After choosing a sort option, the sort control reflects that option as the active/selected value so the user can see which sort is applied.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The active-selection state of the dropdown is a rendered UI property only meaningful in the browser after the interaction; there is no API representation of it.
preconditions: BASE_URL points at Saucedemo; a standard_user session is on the inventory page.

steps.action: Select a non-default sort option (e.g. 'Price (low to high)') in the dropdown.
Inspect the sort control's currently selected value.

expected_results: The sort control shows the chosen option as its active/selected value (it did not reset to the default).
